title: papaver radicatum subsp. stefanssonii
intro: Papaver radicatum subsp. stefanssonii, also known as Stefansson's poppy or Arctic poppy, is a rare and short-lived perennial plant that is native to the Arctic tundra of Greenland and Canada. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and ease muscle spasms. In terms of health benefits, Papaver radicatum subsp. stefanssonii has been traditionally used to treat various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote digestion and relieve menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of the plant, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, but caution is advised due to the plant's potential toxicity and limited scientific research.
